summary: We recommend codifying the three subagents as standalone markdown files under .claude/agents/ rather than inlining their prompts into the heartbeat because each subagent has a distinct trigger and time budget that the orchestrator dispatches independently. The tradeoff is one extra layer of indirection at dispatch time but the gain is that each subagent can evolve without churning the heartbeat prompt. The orchestrator can now invoke outcome-reviewer when a PR opens ci-firefighter when CI goes red and rubric-tightener when an eval results file lands. The README maps trigger to subagent and previews the dispatch json that queue item E4 will ship.

claims.statement: outcome-reviewer subagent file at claude agents outcome-reviewer md reads a PR scores the outcome report pair against HeuristicVerifier and posts one structured review comment with overall dimensions weakest dimension and recommendation lines.

citations.title: outcome-reviewer subagent definition committed in this PR
HeuristicVerifier reference implementation that outcome-reviewer invokes

claims.caveats: The subagent file ships the prompt; actual dispatch from the heartbeat happens via the Agent tool with subagent_type outcome-reviewer.

claims.statement: ci-firefighter subagent file at claude agents ci-firefighter md triages a red check classifies as flake or outcome-gate or typecheck or unknown and fixes mechanical breakage in-place within a 60-second hard budget and escalates otherwise to alex-jadecli.

citations.title: ci-firefighter subagent definition committed in this PR
heartbeat payload that dispatches ci-firefighter on red CI per rule 2

claims.caveats: ci-firefighter never disables the outcome-gate and never force-pushes and never amends commits it didn't author this turn.

claims.statement: rubric-tightener subagent file at claude agents rubric-tightener md reads an eval results file picks the worst dimension by combined false-positive plus false-negative count and proposes one port-stable verifier edit via PR on opensubagents outcomes touching both Python and TypeScript SDKs and a test.

citations.title: rubric-tightener subagent definition committed in this PR
Appendix A reference rubric the tightener edits against

claims.caveats: rubric-tightener depends on queue item D3 having produced at least one results file; until D3 lands the subagent has nothing to do.

open_questions: Should the three subagents be promoted into a sibling opensubagents skills repo so that any Claude session on any project can install them as a plugin or are they intentionally scoped to outcomes-mcp orchestration?
methodology_notes: Three subagent markdown files were authored with the same frontmatter shape as the existing ~/.claude/agents/code-review-expert.md so they slot cleanly into the Claude Code agent loader. The README maps trigger to subagent and previews the dispatch.json that queue item E4 will ship later. Bootstrap pair verified locally against the python HeuristicVerifier before pushing.
